steps.title: Use the angle at line q
steps.content: Angle $b$ is $122°$, so the adjacent angle on line $q$ is $180°-122°=58°$.

steps.title: Find the larger angle at line t
steps.content: Using the small triangle above line $q$, the exterior angle made by line $r$ and the horizontal is $43°+58°=101°$. Since $q \parallel t$, the matching obtuse angle below line $t$ is also $101°$.

steps.title: Use the equal w markings
steps.content: The diagram marks that obtuse angle as two equal angles, each labeled $w$. So $2w=101°$, and $w=\frac{101}{2}$.
